structured_payload_candidate.title: Cooling Mode Primary Airflow Setpoint Mapping
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: When the Zone State Is Cooling
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Parallel fan-powered terminal unit, constant-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When the zone is in cooling state, the cooling-loop output is mapped to the active primary airflow setpoint from the minimum endpoint to the cooling maximum endpoint. If supply air temperature is greater than room temperature, the setpoint is limited to the minimum endpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Map cooling-loop output to active primary airflow setpoint from minimum endpoint to cooling maximum endpoint. If SAT > room temperature, limit setpoint to minimum endpoint. Heating coil OFF.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
When the Zone State Is Cooling
a. The cooling-loop output shall be mapped to the active primary airflow setpoint from the minimum endpoint to the cooling maximum endpoint.
1. If supply air temperature from the air handler is greater than room temperature, the active airflow setpoint shall be no higher than the minimum endpoint.
b. Heating coil is OFF.

evidence_quote:
When the Zone State Is Cooling
a. The cooling-loop output shall be mapped to the active primary airflow setpoint from the minimum endpoint to the cooling maximum endpoint.
1. If supply air temperature from the air handler is greater than room temperature, the active airflow setpoint shall be no higher than the minimum endpoint.
b. Heating coil is OFF.
evidence_text:
When the Zone State Is Cooling
a. The cooling-loop output shall be mapped to the active primary airflow setpoint from the minimum endpoint to the cooling maximum endpoint.
1. If supply air temperature from the air handler is greater than room temperature, the active airflow setpoint shall be no higher than the minimum endpoint.
b. Heating coil is OFF.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Deadband Mode Primary Airflow Setpoint
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: When the Zone State Is Deadband
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Parallel fan-powered terminal unit, constant-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: In deadband, the primary airflow setpoint is the minimum endpoint and the heating coil is off.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Primary airflow setpoint shall be the minimum endpoint. Heating coil OFF.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
When the Zone State Is Deadband
a. The primary airflow setpoint shall be the minimum endpoint.
b. Heating coil is OFF.

evidence_quote:
When the Zone State Is Deadband
a. The primary airflow setpoint shall be the minimum endpoint.
b. Heating coil is OFF.
evidence_text:

b. Heating coil is OFF.

structured_payload_candidate.title: VAV Damper Modulation
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Parallel fan-powered terminal unit, constant-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The VAV damper modulates to maintain measured primary airflow at setpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Always active when terminal unit is operating
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Modulate VAV damper to maintain measured primary airflow at setpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The VAV damper shall be modulated to maintain the measured primary airflow at setpoint.

evidence_quote: The VAV damper shall be modulated to maintain the measured primary airflow at setpoint.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Fan Control During Heating
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Parallel fan-powered terminal unit, constant-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The parallel fan runs whenever the zone state is heating.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Fan shall run whenever Zone State is heating.

evidence_quote: Fan shall run whenever Zone State is heating.
evidence_text: Fan shall run whenever Zone State is heating.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Fan Control for Ventilation (ASHRAE 62.1)
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Parallel fan-powered terminal unit, constant-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If ventilation complies with ASHRAE Standard 62.1, the fan runs in Deadband and Cooling when primary air volume is less than Voz for 1 minute and shuts off when primary air volume is above Voz by 10% for 3 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Fan shall run in Deadband and Cooling when primary air volume is less than Voz for 1 minute and shall shut off when primary air volume is above Voz by 10% for 3 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If ventilation is according to ASHRAE Standard 62.1-2016, the fan shall run in Deadband and Cooling when the primary air volume is less than Voz for 1 minute and shall shut off when primary air volume is above Voz by 10% for 3 minutes.

evidence_quote: If ventilation is according to ASHRAE Standard 62.1-2016, the fan shall run in Deadband and Cooling when the primary air volume is less than Voz for 1 minute and shall shut off when primary air volume is above Voz by 10% for 3 minutes.
evidence_text: If ventilation is according to ASHRAE Standard 62.1-2016, the fan shall run in Deadband and Cooling when the primary air volume is less than Voz for 1 minute and shall shut off when primary air volume is above Voz by 10% for 3 minutes.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Fan Control for Ventilation (California Title 24)
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Parallel fan-powered terminal unit, constant-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If ventilation complies with California Title 24, the fan runs in Deadband and Cooling when primary air volume is less than Zone-Abs-OA-min for 1 minute and shuts off when primary air volume is above Zone-Abs-OA-min by 10% for 3 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Fan shall run in Deadband and Cooling when primary air volume is less than Zone-Abs-OA-min for 1 minute, and shall shut off when primary air volume is above Zone-Abs-OA-min by 10% for 3 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If ventilation is according to California Title 24, the fan shall run in Deadband and Cooling when the primary air volume is less than Zone-Abs-OA-min for 1 minute, and shall shut off when primary air volume is above Zone-Abs-OA-min by 10% for 3 minutes.

evidence_quote: If ventilation is according to California Title 24, the fan shall run in Deadband and Cooling when the primary air volume is less than Zone-Abs-OA-min for 1 minute, and shall shut off when primary air volume is above Zone-Abs-OA-min by 10% for 3 minutes.
evidence_text: If ventilation is according to California Title 24, the fan shall run in Deadband and Cooling when the primary air volume is less than Zone-Abs-OA-min for 1 minute, and shall shut off when primary air volume is above Zone-Abs-OA-min by 10% for 3 minutes.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Low Primary Airflow Alarm (Level 4)
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Parallel fan-powered terminal unit, constant-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Generate a Level 4 alarm if measured airflow is less than 70% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint is greater than zero.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Measured airflow < 70% of setpoint for 10 minutes AND setpoint > 0
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Measured airflow < 70% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint > 0
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If the measured airflow is less than 70% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint is greater than zero, generate a Level 4 alarm.

evidence_quote: If the measured airflow is less than 70% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint is greater than zero, generate a Level 4 alarm.
evidence_text: If the measured airflow is less than 70% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint is greater than zero, generate a Level 4 alarm.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Low Primary Airflow Alarm Suppression
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Parallel fan-powered terminal unit, constant-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Suppress low airflow alarms for zones with Importance-Multiplier of 0 for static pressure reset T&R control loop.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Zone Importance-Multiplier = 0 for static pressure reset T&R control loop
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Suppress low airflow alarms for that zone.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If a zone has an Importance-Multiplier of 0 (see Section 5.1.14.2.a.1) for its static pressure reset T&R control loop, low airflow alarms shall be suppressed for that zone.

evidence_quote: If a zone has an Importance-Multiplier of 0 (see Section 5.1.14.2.a.1) for its static pressure reset T&R control loop, low airflow alarms shall be suppressed for that zone.
evidence_text: If a zone has an Importance-Multiplier of 0 (see Section 5.1.14.2.a.1) for its static pressure reset T&R control loop, low airflow alarms shall be suppressed for that zone.

structured_payload_candidate.summary: Generate a Level 4 alarm if heating hot-water plant is proven ON and DAT is 8.3°C (15°F) less than setpoint for 10 minutes.

structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If heating hot-water plant is proven ON, and the DAT is 8.3°C (15°F) less than setpoint for 10 minutes, generate a Level 4 alarm.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Airflow Sensor Calibration Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Parallel fan-powered terminal unit, constant-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Generate a Level 3 alarm if fan is OFF and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of cooling maximum airflow setpoint or 50 cfm for 30 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Fan OFF AND airflow sensor reading > max(10% of Vcool-max, 50 cfm) for 30 minutes
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Airflow sensor reading above threshold while fan is OFF for 30 minutes
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If the fan serving the zone is OFF and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of the cooling maximum airflow setpoint or 50 cfm for 30 minutes, generate a Level 3 alarm.

evidence_quote: If the fan serving the zone is OFF and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of the cooling maximum airflow setpoint or 50 cfm for 30 minutes, generate a Level 3 alarm.
evidence_text: If the fan serving the zone is OFF and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of the cooling maximum airflow setpoint or 50 cfm for 30 minutes, generate a Level 3 alarm.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Leaking Damper Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Parallel fan-powered terminal unit, constant-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Generate a Level 4 alarm if damper position is 0%, airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of cooling maximum airflow setpoint or 50 cfm for 10 minutes while the fan is proven on.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Damper position = 0% AND airflow sensor reading > max(10% of Vcool-max, 50 cfm) for 10 minutes AND fan proven ON
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Airflow above threshold with damper closed and fan on for 10 minutes
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If the damper position is 0%, and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of the cooling maximum airflow setpoint or 50 cfm for 10 minutes while the fan serving the zone is proven on, generate a Level 4 alarm.

evidence_quote: If the damper position is 0%, and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of the cooling maximum airflow setpoint or 50 cfm for 10 minutes while the fan serving the zone is proven on, generate a Level 4 alarm.
evidence_text: If the damper position is 0%, and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of the cooling maximum airflow setpoint or 50 cfm for 10 minutes while the fan serving the zone is proven on, generate a Level 4 alarm.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Leaking Valve Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Parallel fan-powered terminal unit, constant-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Generate a Level 4 alarm if valve position is 0% for 15 minutes, DAT is above AHU SAT by 3°C (5°F), and the fan is proven ON.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Valve position = 0% for 15 minutes AND DAT > AHU SAT + 3°C (5°F) AND fan proven ON
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Valve closed but DAT elevated above AHU SAT with fan on for 15 minutes
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If the valve position is 0% for 15 minutes, DAT is above AHU SAT by 3°C (5°F), and the fan serving the zone is proven ON, generate a Level 4 alarm.

evidence_quote: If the valve position is 0% for 15 minutes, DAT is above AHU SAT by 3°C (5°F), and the fan serving the zone is proven ON, generate a Level 4 alarm.
evidence_text: If the valve position is 0% for 15 minutes, DAT is above AHU SAT by 3°C (5°F), and the fan serving the zone is proven ON, generate a Level 4 alarm.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Testing/Commissioning Overrides
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Parallel fan-powered terminal unit, constant-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Provide software switches interlocked to a system level point to force zone airflow setpoint to zero, Vcool-max, Vmin; force damper full closed/open; force heating OFF/closed; turn fan ON/OFF; and reset request-hours accumulator point to zero.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior:
Provide software switches that interlock to a system level point to:
a. force zone airflow setpoint to zero,
b. force zone airflow setpoint to Vcool-max,
c. force zone airflow setpoint to Vmin,
d. force damper full closed/open,
e. force heating to OFF/closed,
f. turn fan ON/OFF, and
g. reset request-hours accumulator point to zero (provide one point for each reset type listed in the next section).
structured_payload_candidate.commissioning_check: Verify that software switches exist and can override the listed points.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
Provide software switches that interlock to a system level point to
a. force zone airflow setpoint to zero,
b. force zone airflow setpoint to Vcool-max,
c. force zone airflow setpoint to Vmin,
d. force damper full closed/open,
e. force heating to OFF/closed,
f. turn fan ON/OFF, and
g. reset request-hours accumulator point to zero (provide one point for each reset type listed in the next section).

evidence_quote:
Provide software switches that interlock to a system level point to
a. force zone airflow setpoint to zero,
b. force zone airflow setpoint to Vcool-max,
c. force zone airflow setpoint to Vmin,
d. force damper full closed/open,
e. force heating to OFF/closed,
f. turn fan ON/OFF, and
g. reset request-hours accumulator point to zero (provide one point for each reset type listed in the next section).
evidence_text:
Provide software switches that interlock to a system level point to

structured_payload_candidate.title: Endpoints as a Function of Zone Group Mode
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Endpoints as a Function of Zone Group Mode
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Parallel fan-powered terminal unit, constant-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Cooling maximum and minimum airflow endpoints vary by Zone Group mode: Occupied/Cooldown/Setup use Vcool-max and Vmin*; Warmup/Setback/Unoccupied use 0 for both.
configurable_values: Vmin (zone minimum primary airflow setpoint, may be AUTO)
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
Table 5.7.4 Endpoints as a Function of Zone Group Mode
Endpoint Occupied Cooldown Setup Warmup Setback Unoccupied
Cooling maximum Vcool-max Vcool-max Vcool-max 0 0 0
Minimum Vmin* 0 0 0 0 0
